symptoms.symptom_name: Syncope while supine and asleep (loss of consciousness at rest)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Syncope at rest/supine strongly favours an arrhythmic cause over reflex or orthostatic syncope.
Reflex syncope essentially does not occur while lying flat and asleep.
Agonal breathing is a recognised sign of cerebral hypoperfusion during a malignant arrhythmia / cardiac arrest.
Gasping can occur in OSA, but not as an isolated acute collapse with loss of consciousness.

symptoms.symptom_name: First-degree relative with premature sudden unexplained death in sleep (brother, age 30)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Premature sudden death in a first-degree relative is a strong pointer to a heritable arrhythmia syndrome such as Brugada or long-QT.
Fever unmasks the type-1 Brugada ECG pattern and precipitates ventricular arrhythmia; it is a classic trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of sustained tonic-clonic activity, tongue-biting or incontinence; brief post-event confusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lack of tonic-clonic movement, tongue-biting and a prolonged post-ictal phase makes a primary seizure less likely and favours cardiac syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: No preceding chest pain, palpitations or prodrome; no prior syncope

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden arrhythmic syncope during sleep characteristically has no prodrome; absence of warning does not lower the risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Structurally normal heart on examination (normal S1/S2, no murmurs, heaves or thrills)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Channelopathies characteristically occur in a structurally normal heart; a normal cardiac exam does not exclude and is consistent with an inherited electrical disorder. Clinician-only finding — sealed.
Absence of a murmur or heave makes an obstructive structural cause less likely, though imaging is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG with high right precordial leads showing coved type-1 ST-segment elevation in V1–V3

investigation_details.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG (with high right precordial leads), ideally during fever
investigation_details.expected_result: Coved (type-1) ST-segment elevation ≥2 mm in one or more of V1–V3 — diagnostic of the Brugada pattern
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without an ECG under monitoring the diagnosis is missed and a recurrent lethal arrhythmia can occur undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A spontaneous or fever-induced type-1 coved pattern in the right precordial leads is diagnostic of Brugada syndrome. Hospital investigation — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Continuous cardiac telemetry capturing polymorphic VT / VF

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continuous monitoring is needed to detect a recurrent malignant arrhythmia; its absence does not reassure. Hospital-only.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Aborted sudden cardiac arrest / inherited channelopathy
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Supine/asleep syncope + agonal breathing + familial premature sudden death is a red-flag triad that mandates emergency cardiac monitoring regardless of a well-appearing patient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate 12-lead ECG under continuous cardiac monitoring (hospital)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important investigation — captures the diagnostic Brugada pattern and detects recurrent arrhythmia. Requires emergency department transfer.
Detects recurrent polymorphic VT/VF while the febrile trigger persists.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes (potassium, calcium, magnesium) and cardiac biomarkers
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes electrolyte-provoked arrhythmia and myocardial injury as contributors.
Confirms a structurally normal heart and excludes cardiomyopathy as an alternative SCD substrate.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine EEG / outpatient sleep study as the primary work-up
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Anchoring on a seizure or sleep-apnoea work-up before excluding a cardiac arrhythmic cause is the classic error here and dangerously delays cardiac monitoring.
